title: Part 3 – Entraînement 3 : conversations divertissement & santé
instructions: Écoutez chaque conversation, puis répondez aux trois questions.

items.audioText: Man: Are you coming to the charity gala on Saturday evening? Woman: I'd like to, but I'm not sure I have anything appropriate to wear. What's the dress code? Man: Black tie. It's quite formal. But the event is for a great cause — they're raising money for the children's hospital. Woman: In that case, I'll make the effort. How much are the tickets? Man: Two hundred euros per person, but the company is covering half for each employee who attends.

questions.prompt: What event is being discussed?
questions.explanation: "Are you coming to the charity gala on Saturday evening?"

questions.prompt: What is the dress code?
questions.explanation: "Black tie. It's quite formal".

questions.prompt: How much will each employee pay after the company contribution?
questions.explanation: Tickets cost 200 euros and the company covers half, so employees pay 100 euros.

items.audioText: Woman: I booked a table at Maison Verte for the client dinner on Thursday. It's that new French restaurant near the station. Man: I've heard great things about it. How many will we be? Woman: Six — the two of us, two clients, and their director. Man: Should I arrange transportation? Some of the clients are coming from the airport. Woman: Good thinking. Please book a taxi to pick them up at seven and bring them to the restaurant. The reservation is at eight.

questions.prompt: Where has the woman booked a table?
choices: An Italian restaurant near the office
A French restaurant near the station called Maison Verte
A sushi restaurant in the city centre
questions.explanation: "I booked a table at Maison Verte... that new French restaurant near the station".

questions.prompt: How many people will attend the dinner?
questions.explanation: "Six — the two of us, two clients, and their director".

questions.prompt: What time should the taxi pick up the clients?
questions.explanation: "Please book a taxi to pick them up at seven".

items.audioText: Man: I need to take tomorrow afternoon off. I have a dental appointment that can't be postponed. Woman: That should be fine. Is it a routine check-up or something more serious? Man: Just a check-up and a possible filling. I should be done by four. Woman: In that case, could you join the three-thirty call remotely if you're available? Man: I'll try. But if the appointment runs late, I'll dial in from the waiting room.

questions.prompt: Why does the man need tomorrow afternoon off?
choices: He needs to collect his children
questions.explanation: "I have a dental appointment that can't be postponed".

questions.prompt: What time does the man expect to finish his appointment?
questions.explanation: "I should be done by four".

questions.prompt: What does the woman ask the man to do if he's available?
questions.explanation: "could you join the three-thirty call remotely if you're available?"

items.audioText: Woman: I'm organising the company summer party. Do you think we should do something indoors or outdoors this year? Man: Given last year's weather disaster, I'd go indoors. Maybe hire a venue with a rooftop terrace as a backup option. Woman: I was thinking the same. I've found a great events space — it has a large hall and a terrace. It holds up to two hundred and fifty people. Man: Perfect. When are you planning to hold it? Woman: The last Friday of June. I'm hoping to confirm the venue by the end of this week.

questions.prompt: What is the woman organising?
questions.explanation: "I'm organising the company summer party".

questions.prompt: How many people can the venue accommodate?
choices: Up to one hundred and fifty
Up to two hundred and fifty
questions.explanation: "It holds up to two hundred and fifty people".

questions.prompt: When does the woman plan to confirm the venue?
choices: By the end of this week
By the end of the month
By the end of next week
questions.explanation: "I'm hoping to confirm the venue by the end of this week".

items.audioText: Man: I've been feeling really stressed lately. I don't think I'm managing my workload well. Woman: Have you spoken to your manager about it? Man: Not yet. I'm worried it will look like I can't cope. Woman: I understand that concern, but it's better to address it early. You could also try the company's Employee Assistance Programme — they offer free counselling sessions. Man: I didn't know about that. How do I access it? Woman: There's a dedicated phone line. I'll send you the number now.

questions.prompt: What problem does the man describe?
choices: Feeling stressed and struggling with his workload
A health issue requiring time off
questions.explanation: "I've been feeling really stressed lately. I don't think I'm managing my workload well".

questions.prompt: Why hasn't the man spoken to his manager?
choices: He is afraid of looking unable to cope
He prefers to handle things alone
questions.explanation: "I'm worried it will look like I can't cope".

questions.prompt: What does the Employee Assistance Programme offer?
questions.explanation: "they offer free counselling sessions".

items.audioText: Woman: I'm thinking of signing up for the lunchtime yoga classes the company is offering. Have you tried them? Man: Yes, I've been going every Tuesday for a month now. They're excellent. The instructor is very good at adapting to different fitness levels. Woman: That's reassuring — I haven't done yoga in a while. Is it held in the building? Man: Yes, in the wellness room on the ground floor. You need to book your spot in advance through the intranet. Woman: Great, I'll register for next Tuesday's session.

questions.prompt: What class is the woman thinking of joining?
questions.explanation: "I'm thinking of signing up for the lunchtime yoga classes".

questions.prompt: Where are the classes held?
choices: At a gym near the office
In the wellness room on the ground floor
questions.explanation: "in the wellness room on the ground floor".

questions.prompt: How do employees book their spot?
choices: By signing up at the door
questions.explanation: "You need to book your spot in advance through the intranet".

items.audioText: Man: Did you hear that the company is offering subsidised gym memberships starting next month? Woman: Really? Which gym? Man: Any of the three partner gyms in the city. The company covers sixty percent of the monthly fee. Woman: That's a great benefit. Is there a maximum number of memberships available? Man: The first fifty employees to register get it. After that, it goes on a waiting list. Woman: I should sign up right away then. Where do I register? Man: Through HR — just fill in the form on the intranet.

questions.prompt: What benefit is the company introducing?
questions.explanation: "the company is offering subsidised gym memberships starting next month".

questions.prompt: What percentage of the membership fee does the company cover?
questions.explanation: "The company covers sixty percent of the monthly fee".

questions.prompt: What happens after the first fifty registrations?
choices: Employees go on a waiting list
The company fee drops to forty percent
questions.explanation: "After that, it goes on a waiting list".

items.audioText: Woman: I wanted to let you know that the office first aid kit needs restocking. The bandages and pain relief tablets are almost gone. Man: Thanks for flagging that. I'll order new supplies today. Is anything else missing? Woman: I noticed there are no latex gloves either. We should have at least two pairs. Man: Noted. I'll add those to the order. Do you know if our first aid certificate is still valid? Woman: I'm not sure. I think it was renewed last year but we should double-check. Man: I'll look it up and let you know.

questions.prompt: What does the woman report?
choices: The first aid kit needs restocking
questions.explanation: "the office first aid kit needs restocking".

questions.prompt: What else is missing from the kit?
questions.explanation: "there are no latex gloves either".

questions.prompt: What will the man check?
choices: The expiry dates on all first aid items
Whether the first aid certificate is still valid
How many employees are first aid trained
Which supplier to use for restocking
questions.explanation: "I'll look it up and let you know" (whether the first aid certificate is still valid).

items.audioText: Man: We've just heard that the industry awards ceremony is next month. Are we submitting an entry this year? Woman: I think we should. Our packaging redesign project would be a strong candidate for the innovation award. Man: Agreed. Who would write the submission? Woman: I'd suggest asking Clara from the design team — she led the project and knows every detail. Man: Good idea. The deadline is the twenty-second. Can you check with her this week whether she's willing to take it on? Woman: I'll talk to her this afternoon.

questions.prompt: What are the speakers considering entering?
questions.explanation: "the industry awards ceremony is next month. Are we submitting an entry this year?"

questions.prompt: Which project would they submit for the innovation award?
questions.explanation: "Our packaging redesign project would be a strong candidate for the innovation award".

questions.prompt: When will the woman speak to Clara?
questions.explanation: "I'll talk to her this afternoon".

items.audioText: Woman: I'm preparing the welcome speech for the new employees joining next week. Any suggestions on what to include? Man: Definitely cover the company values and culture — new hires always say that's what they care most about. Woman: Good point. I was also thinking of sharing a few highlights from our recent achievements. Man: Great idea. Keep it under ten minutes though — people tend to switch off after that. Woman: Noted. I'll also leave time for a brief Q&A at the end so they feel welcome to ask questions. Man: Perfect. That personal touch makes a big difference.

questions.prompt: What is the woman preparing?
choices: A welcome speech for new employees
questions.explanation: "I'm preparing the welcome speech for the new employees joining next week".

questions.prompt: How long does the man recommend the speech should be?
questions.explanation: "Keep it under ten minutes though — people tend to switch off after that".

questions.prompt: What does the woman plan to add at the end?
questions.explanation: "I'll also leave time for a brief Q&A at the end".
